     The Numbers Business Report

The Way Back (2020)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $13,590,514Details
International Box Office $1,900,000
Worldwide Box Office $15,490,514
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $1,589,914
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $1,418,488
Total $3,008,402
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Jack Cunningham once had a life filled with promise. In high school, he was a basketball phenom with a full university scholarship, when suddenly, for reasons unknown, he walked away from the game, forfeiting his future. Now years later, Jack is spiraling down, triggered by an unspeakable loss, and drowning in the alcoholism that cost him his marriage and any hope for a better life. When he is asked to coach the basketball team at his alma mater, which has fallen far since his glory days, he reluctantly accepts, surprising no one more than himself. As the boys start to come together as a team and win, Jack may have finally found a reason to confront the demons that have derailed him. But will it be enough to fill the void, heal the deep wounds of his past, and set him on the road to redemption?

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$8,170,315 (60.1% of total gross)
Legs:1.66 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:87.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$23,000,000 (worldwide box office is 0.7 times production budget)
Theater counts:2,718 opening theaters/2,718 max. theaters, 2.0 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $17,706,158

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 6th, 2020 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: March 5th, 2020 (Wide) (New Zealand)
March 6th, 2020 (Wide) (Australia)
March 6th, 2020 (Wide), released as Извън играта (Bulgaria)
March 6th, 2020 (Wide), released as IZVEN IGRIŠČA (Slovenia)
March 13th, 2020 (Wide), released as O Caminho de Volta (Portugal)
Video Release: March 24th, 2020 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: R for language throughout including some sexual references.
(Rating bulletin 2587 (Cert #52285), 7/17/2019)
Running Time: 108 minutes
Plot point: Addiction, Basketball, Inspirational Coach, Inspirational Sports, Redemption, Rehab / Former Addict
Subgenre: Sports Drama
Source:Original Screenplay
Genre:Drama
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Contemporary Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Mayhem Pictures, Bron Creative, Warner Bros., Jennifer Todd Pictures, Film Tribe
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

Jack Cunningham once had a life filled with promise. In high school, he was a basketball phenom with a full university scholarship, when suddenly, for reasons unknown, he walked away from the game, forfeiting his future. Now years later, Jack is spiraling down, triggered by an unspeakable loss, and drowning in the alcoholism that cost him his marriage and any hope for a better life. When he is asked to coach the basketball team at his alma mater, which has fallen far since his glory days, he reluctantly accepts, surprising no one more than himself. As the boys start to come together as a team and win, Jack may have finally found a reason to confront the demons that have derailed him. But will it be enough to fill the void, heal the deep wounds of his past, and set him on the road to redemption?
